text: Financial risks include leverage and liquidity concerns. As of September 2025, Apple had total debt of $112.377B (chunk_id_d534961f-b46e-4c31-9c77-a60ee0ed39e7), representing a debt-to-equity ratio of 2.61 (calc_id_6043888a-b26e-4615-a3a4-fb91b9a2f13b). While this level of debt is manageable, it increases financial risk, especially if interest rates rise. Furthermore, the current ratio of 0.88 (calc_id_6043888a-b26e-4615-a3a4-fb91b9a2f13b) suggests potential liquidity challenges, particularly if short-term liabilities increase. These risks are classified as medium given the company's overall financial strength but require monitoring.
